tokens: And, Dave, give us a call as you come up toward Spur...
transcript.speaker: Allen

transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I think we should work down to the northern rim, right?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, if we're going to sample any blocks there on the rim, that'd be the place to do it.

tokens: And, Dave, we'd like for you to park east of the area you're going to be working in, so we can look down-Sun.

tokens: (Obviously pleased with the way things are going) Okay!
(Pause) That one wall there has quite a bit of debris, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, and it looks like it, again, has a linear pattern running north and south.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (To Joe) We're talking about the debris that's exposed on the north wall of Spur.

transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay; I'll park it east on a level slope here.
(Long Pause) Right down by all the (Laughing) Try to avoid the big crater.
Be a nice place to park.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Take a break here (by working on a level surface for a change).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Okay, I think we're - oop - just about level, right there.

tokens: I'll give them a reading if you want, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yes, I'll get the TV on.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll give them the shadow device, too.

tokens: Copy, and standing by for the (Sun) shadow.

tokens: (Before he hears Joe) And the shadow is 4 degrees left.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yes, the pitch is about 3 degrees down, and the roll...(Pause)

tokens: You have to hold it in the damp position, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: If I can get the...There, it's damping.

tokens: I'm off and I'm going to take a pan.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, I'm off and I'll get the TV.

tokens: And we're looking at about 30 minutes working time here at this station.

tokens: Stand where you are and give me alignment on the antenna, and see if I can get pointed at the Earth.

tokens: ...Dave and Jim, you could look at the meter on the
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: You can use that sound to lock in, Dave.

tokens: Well, it might be the EV visor that makes it look green.

tokens: Notice that large rock on the northwest side, just on the inner edge there

tokens: How about that, We'll get that one.

On top of it is about a 2- to 3-inch subangular frag with a light gray or medium gray matrix, and about 20 percent white clast in it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You're going to sample that, right?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, there are sparklies in...No, it's a breccia.

tokens: I even see some vesicles in it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just standing by for the (bag) number, Dave.

tokens: Standing by for the number, Jim.

tokens: It was under it wasn't it?

tokens: Okay, let me get a(n "after") picture.

tokens: You mean...Well, look at this one right here!

tokens: (Laughing) I've got to admit it really looks green to me, too, Jim, but I can't believe it's green.

tokens: I hope it is green when we get it home.

tokens: (Quite excited and pleased) I told you it was green!!

tokens: Wait a minute, I can't put this into the bag yet; I got to look at this.

tokens: No, I know; I put my visor up, too.

tokens: But it's a very light grey, very fine grained, sure looks like a basalt with some less than millimeter-size vesicles in it, maybe 5 percent or so.

Let me get another one here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, it sounds green to me.

tokens: Here's another one of the same stuff, Jim.

tokens: Why don't you just scoop in between them.

tokens: I think this is a big frag here; but, it broke...

tokens: Get a little (garbled), just for drill.

tokens: Wouldn't you agree with that, Jim?

tokens: (Pause) Here's some dense...And there's another unusual one; look at the little crater here, and the one that's facing us.

tokens: Okay, sounds like a good place....

tokens: Okay, I have my pictures, Dave.

tokens: Let's go get some more of that.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think we ought to get over to that big rock.

tokens: Dave, we think you might be about to run out of film.

tokens: And if you could put that (pedestal sample) in my bag and then check my film.

tokens: And there might be diamonds in the next one.

tokens: Oh, he's got a lot left.

Okay, I have...Oh, look at this, Jim!

tokens: I got about a 4 incher, Joe.
It's subrounded, and on one half of it, we have a very dark, black, fine-grained basalt with some - it looks like some very thin laths in it of plage.
And, in one region, there is some millimeter-type vesicles along a linear pattern very close to the contact.
And, the other side of the contact, we have a pure, solid-white, fine-grained frag, which looks not unlike the white clasts in the 14 rock.
But it's a beautiful contact in here.
And, we'll call this one bag number...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Jim, you may have dropped your sample bags.

tokens: He dropped one, Joe, thank you.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I don't know what we would do without you, Joe.

tokens: We'll ease over to that big rock.

tokens: Here's another clod that evidently hit.

tokens: Yeah, I will as soon as I zip it.

tokens: I was wondering whether you wanted to use the scoop.

tokens: ...we're very pleased with your documented samples here.

tokens: We got to go over and sample that big one there.

tokens: And, Dave, you're going to want to cinch up Jim's collection bag...

tokens: Okay, turn a little right, Jimmy.

tokens: Oh, yeah, that getting in that Rover bit.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can fix you here, partner.

tokens: Okay, Dave, while you're working there, we're thinking that we'd prefer just a very quick sampling of the large rock, if at all.

tokens: Wait Jim; don't go anywhere yet.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave and Jim, the science input now is that we want to forget that large block entirely.
We want as large a collection of smaller frags as you can get us, and you'll probably be working near the Rover for those.

tokens: (Still working on the bottom attachment of Jim's SCB) Understand, Joe.

tokens: Okay, let me get your...I knocked your scoop over.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think it looks very much like the 14 rocks.

tokens: Though, it looks maybe a little darker gray.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm set up whenever you are.

tokens: (Pause) There's a convenient piece broken off, right here (Pause)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, and while I'm raking, there's a rock over there that has a linear pattern in it, that you might want to look at while I'm raking.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Still running toward the Rover) Okay, let me get the pictures of the place.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Jim, how's your raking going?

tokens: (Still running) This level ground is great.

tokens: Got to document the area first here, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: How about right about here, huh?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, that's what I was thinking.
You see that rock over at your...just a little south of you?

tokens: Oh, I just ran out of film.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, we can get that later.
Let me change film mags while you rake, Jim.

tokens: I'm surprised you're running out already, though you must have taken a lot of pictures over there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...our TV camera's starting to warm up considerably.
We'd like for you to clean off the camera top and the LCRU when convenient, please.

tokens: Log bag 171 for a frag off of that big boulder, I'm pretty sure.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And I think I'll brush off the (TV) camera for you, and I can brush off my camera before I change the film.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Joe, this looks like a pretty good place to rake.
I've raked one swath here about 2 feet long and I've collected, oh, about 15 rocks.

tokens: Put them in a big pile; I'll be right over.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, I don't know whether I want to do that, Dave.

tokens: Well, then, I'll be right over.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Though I think we can fill up the bag pretty fast, here.

tokens: Okay, then, you take the pictures and I'll just change my film later.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Save the (the time that would be taken by) film changing here.
Let me get you a bag.

tokens: It (meaning the first swath)'s about 1 meter long and one rake-width wide.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Most of them are rounded; right size.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Oh, you're stepping on my tongs.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can get them with the scoop here in a little bit.

tokens: (Jim finishes the third swatch) Oh, good!
That's three swaths 1 meter long apiece.
(Pause as Jim makes the third pour) And the bag isn't full yet.
Let's shoot for a full bag.
(It will) take you just a second to go one more sweep there.
(Pause as Jim starts the fourth swath) Oh, my poor old tong!

tokens: Oh, I don't weigh that much up here, Dave, to break your tongs.

tokens: I don't know what I'd do without them.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just so I don't bury them.

tokens: Don't shake anymore, you('ll lose some of the rocks)...(Pause) Yeah.
That's too bad; we didn't get many out of that one.
Why don't you take one over...Let me move the gnomon about 3 inches here; and take one on this side, Jim.
Move the gnomon back about a foot.
Why don't you take a swath here and I'll...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, you know, because we're moving a little farther from the rim
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We get less and less each swath.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: This one ought to be a more fruitful one.

tokens: (Pause while Jim takes the final swath) (To Houston) How about a double core here, Joe?

tokens: (While Jim makes the pour) Dave, we're coming up on the departure time about 10 minutes from now.

tokens: (Closing the bag) We got a whole bagful of those in the comp(rehensive rake sample).

tokens: I guess all we need is a soil sample from this area and perhaps even larger rocks, if there's some grapefruit to football-size rocks there.

tokens: (Pause) Yeah, we'll just finish off Jim's collection bag here.

tokens: Keep an eye on the spherule.

tokens: So I'll get you a bag; (and) let you take a picture of that.

tokens: Where you going to put that little spherule?

tokens: Not with the soil, though, are you?

tokens: I just didn't want to miss it.

tokens: And we want to leave in about...

tokens: (Joking) Is that a full bag there?

tokens: I want to see you shake that one down.

tokens: Okay; that doesn't give us time to really do much then.

tokens: Okay, I haven't secured the rake there, yet.

tokens: You secure the rake, and I'll secure the TV, and we'll get moving.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I wonder how I'm going to do this (that is, stow the rock).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...the TV camera where it is right now.
It's stowed in the proper position.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Any place we can put your bag on it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Why don't you put that in one of those bags, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm going to put it in the seat pan now.
And then, why don't you put your bag in here.
Here, let me have...Come over here.
I'm going to put your bag in there.
Your carrier is awful loose, and I don't want to lose that bag.
Put this on the Hand Tool Carrier.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, I don't know what's in there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: How many big rocks did you pick up?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I think that's our best...(Stops to listen to Joe)
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We're about out of time, here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Although we're not hurting all that bad, but...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Probably getting Jim's bag off) Bend over, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Looks like you really put some weight on our suspension system when you loaded it, there.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: [Chuckles] Wait until you feel this bag!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It'll weigh even more when you get it home.
And we're interested in getting you to move on out now, troops.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Going as fast as we can, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Okay, you want to just stay in
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Go ahead and stow it (meaning the high-gain antenna).
We just don't want you to move the television camera.
It's in the proper position now.
We want you to go ahead and stow the antenna.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Better let me get in first, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And don't forget the ignition key.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay; the brush is going to be under my seat pan.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Standing by for your heading reading.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (With a slight scolding tone) Okay; stand by, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Are you going to give us an update, Joe?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You want me to torque it, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yes, go ahead; and then...Oh, shoot!
You better get in so I can get your...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...If we're going downslope, I'll just hang on.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, that looks good (meaning that Jim is properly seated).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Shoot, I didn't put it down (between the seats).
I didn't think I was going to use it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You can't get it up high enough to use it.
That's part of the problem: the scoop holds it way down.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hope you can get your feet in there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, are you hard to put to bed!
Could you bring your left leg over, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: When we leave here, I'm in a position to shoot some film.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We'll get some downhill motion, here (meaning some movie footage of the Rover traveling downhill.) (Pause)
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Long Pause) Did you torque it, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can get it now if you like.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Dave, we want you to head toward Station 4, and we'll advise you on what your rate looks like and the tasks that we want you to carry out once you arrive.
Just start off in the direction of Station 4, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I can see it over there, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That's not going to mean much to you until you get down to the level.
transcript.speaker: Irwin
